mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals expanding labor income and improving corporate revenue expectations, causing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the payroll trend is rising.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot was 132989 thousand persons as of one month before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a labor-market deterioration that lowers expected earnings growth and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot was 8.1 percent as of one month before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or if average triggered returns are not below non-triggered returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25 percent and INDPRO is rising over three months, accommodative policy combined with real activity improvement compresses discount rates and supports positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25 and INDPRO 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:INDPRO
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot was 0.14 percent as of one month before 2012-05-15
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot was 96.5685 as of two months before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and rising-INDPRO condition does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than other periods in walk-forward tests or if its hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in RSAFS indicates strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed companies, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the retail-sales signal is observed.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate crosses above its prior 6-month average growth rate
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot was 408039 million dollars as of one month before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS acceleration events are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if event-window mean returns are not positive at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST reflects improving construction activity, credit transmission, and household balance-sheet confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month moving average crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest snapshot was 654 thousand units as of two months before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST moving-average crossover is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are not above unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO over three months signals weakening production, lower capacity use, and deteriorating earnings momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot was 96.5685 as of two months before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or if the signal has nonnegative Rank IC over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises faster than 0.5 percent over three months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent, real policy rates fall and inflation risk is tolerated, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot was 229.177 as of one month before 2012-05-15
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot was 0.14 percent as of one month before 2012-05-15
regime_hint: financial repression with anchored short rates
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-CPI-growth and near-zero-FEDFUNDS condition has lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than all other observations or if its positive-return hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and UNRATE is falling over three months, broad demand expansion and labor improvement jointly raise expected earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:UNRATE
fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot was 13502.4 billions of chained 2017 dollars as of four months before 2012-05-15
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot was 8.1 percent as of one month before 2012-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if this joint demand-labor improvement condition fails to outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests or if positive-return frequency is below 55%.
